title: polylepis besseri subsp. incarum
intro: Polylepis besseri subsp. incarum, also known as Queñua or Inkari, is a type of tree found in the Andean region of South America, specifically in Peru, Bolivia, and Ecuador, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The bark and leaves of this tree have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Polylepis besseri subsp. incarum include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds and infections, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine in the Andean region. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to promote overall well-being and combat illnesses.
